text:
In Class
IX, you
have studied that to locate
the
position of a point on a p
plane, we
require a pair of coordinate axes. The distance of a point f
from the y-axis is called its
x-coordinate, or abscissa. T
The distance of a point
from
the x-axis
s is called its
y-coordinate, or ordinate. The coordinates of a point on t
the x-axis
are of the form
(x, O), and of a point on the y-axis are of the form
(0, y).
